title: Validating HTML Anchor Tags with Strict Tag Names

statement.markdown: You are given a string representing the HTML anchor tags that need to be validated. The task is to check if all anchor tags in the string have a valid tag name (a, area, link) and a corresponding closing tag. A valid tag name is one that matches the pattern a|area|link. The function should return True if all anchor tags are valid and False otherwise. The input string may contain multiple anchor tags, and the tags may be nested within other HTML tags.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the HTML anchor tags.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether all anchor tags are valid.
statement.notes: This problem requires a deep understanding of HTML parsing and tag validation.

constraints.description: The maximum number of HTML tags in the input string.

examples.explanation: The anchor tag is valid and has a corresponding closing tag.
The anchor tag is missing a closing tag.
The area tag is not a valid anchor tag.

hints.text: Use a stack data structure to keep track of opening tags.
Check if the closing tag matches the opening tag.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to use a stack to keep track of opening tags and their corresponding closing tags.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to use a regular expression to match all anchor tags and then check if they are valid.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a stack to keep track of opening tags and then checks if the closing tag matches the opening tag.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the stack data structure and the matching of opening and closing tags.
commonMistakes: Not using a stack to keep track of opening tags
Not checking if the closing tag matches the opening tag
